title: ProcessOwnershipLedger Postgres primitive (table + write path)
goal: Land the kernel_process_lifecycle Postgres table and the perf-safe batched/async write path for ProcessOwnershipLedger (AC-PROCESS-LEDGER-PRIMITIVE + AC-PROCESS-LEDGER-PERF). Every process Handshake spawns (model engine, mechanical job, ASR worker, ComfyUI worker, sandbox container, plugin process, helper subprocess) writes START + STOP rows; no exceptions. The write path uses a bounded mpsc channel so spawn threads never block on Postgres.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/{mod.rs,table.rs,writer.rs}; SQL migration file under existing migrations dir; tests proving START+STOP semantics and channel-saturation degraded-mode receipt.
unblock_conditions: MT-004 handoff-gate exists (ledger writes integrate with handoff gate evidence pointers); MT-006 violation receipt schema exists (degraded-mode emits a typed FR-EVT-LEDGER-OVERFLOW receipt).
implementation_notes: Per spec §5.7.1 (AC-PROCESS-LEDGER-PRIMITIVE) the Postgres table `kernel_process_lifecycle` has columns: process_uuid UUID PRIMARY KEY (v7 per HBR-INT-008), parent_session_id TEXT, sandbox_adapter_id TEXT, engine_kind TEXT NOT NULL (enum text: 'llamacpp'|'candle'|'abliteration_tool'|'sandbox_container'|'mechanical_job'|'asr_worker'|'comfyui_worker'|'plugin_process'|'helper_subprocess'|'external_compat'), started_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, stopped_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL, exit_code INTEGER NULL, model_artifact_sha256 TEXT NULL, work_profile_id TEXT NULL, owner_role TEXT NOT NULL, owner_wp TEXT NULL. Indexes on (parent_session_id, started_at) and (engine_kind, started_at). SQL migration file goes under the existing migrations directory (find via `Glob src/backend/**/migrations/**` before authoring; if absent, create src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/ and document the choice in implementation_notes of the next MT). Per spec §5.7.3 (AC-PROCESS-LEDGER-PERF) the writer uses a bounded `tokio::sync::mpsc::channel::<LedgerEvent>(capacity)` where capacity is configurable per Work Profile (default 1024). The spawn thread calls `writer.append_start(event)` which returns `Ok(())` immediately after a non-blocking `try_send`; on channel full, emit a typed `FR-EVT-LEDGER-OVERFLOW` event (using MT-006 receipt format with violation_class=MATRIX_SCHEMA_VIOLATION repurposed — actually add a new EventLedger event kind FR_EVT_LEDGER_OVERFLOW carrying `{ overflow_count, capacity, dropped_event_kind, sampled_event_payload }`) and set a degraded-mode flag readable by the Diagnostics panel (Diagnostics panel wiring is cluster X scope; this MT only sets the flag and exposes it via `process_ledger::is_degraded() -> bool`). NEVER silently drop: every overflow MUST emit the typed event. A dedicated writer task consumes the channel and batches inserts (batch size 32, flush interval 100ms) via sqlx::query. Per spec §5.7.5 (AC-PROCESS-LEDGER-RECLAIM) the reclaim path is a separate MT (MT-008); this MT only ships START/STOP API + perf-safe writer + overflow handling. Tests: stress test fires 10k spawns into a capacity-128 channel and asserts (a) no spawn blocks > 1ms, (b) overflow event emitted with correct overflow_count, (c) degraded-mode flag flips true and back false after drain; correctness test asserts each START has a matching STOP after a process exits.
risk_if_missed: Without the perf-safe writer, swarm-N spawn bursts (HBR-SWARM-001) block on Postgres and starve the sandbox layer; ledger becomes a bottleneck.
validator_focus: Stress test shows no spawn-thread blocking; overflow emits a typed event (not a silent drop); STOP row is always written even when START hit overflow.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Stress-test asserts spawn threads never block > 1ms under 10k burst.
Overflow path emits a typed event (never silent drop).
process_uuid uses Uuid::now_v7() and the test asserts monotonicity across two sequential mints (HBR-INT-008).
Per CX-503R no SQLite anywhere: this MT must add zero new SQLite dependencies; assert via cargo tree grep test.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Postgres table + perf-safe batched async writer (mpsc::channel + JoinHandle + ticker flush) is well-structured with degraded-mode signaling, overflow event emission, and Uuid::now_v7() for process_uuid + overflow_uuid. However flush failures in run_writer (lines 347, 352) use `let _ = flush_batch(...).await` which SILENTLY DISCARDS write errors — the batch is cleared on success but on error the batch is held with no retry and no logging, just an internal degraded flag.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Postgres table + perf-safe batched async writer (mpsc::channel + JoinHandle + ticker flush) is well-structured with degraded-mode signaling, overflow event emission, and Uuid::now_v7() for process_uuid + overflow_uuid. However flush failures in run_writer (lines 347, 352) use `let _ = flush_batch(...).await` which SILENTLY DISCARDS write errors — the batch is cleared on success but on error the batch is held with no retry and no logging, just an internal degraded flag.
